comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text:
Working on Phase 1: Sort Conditions.

Progress so far:
- Gherkin spec already existed (features/sort/sort_conditions.feature) — 9 operators covered with Scenario Outlines, null handling, empty string edge cases
- Added Scenario Outline for null input across all 8 single-value operators (was only testing eq before; needed to reach 100% coverage)
- Python: sort.py implemented with Sort class, all 9 operators (_coerce helper for numeric string coercion). 100% coverage. Black + Ruff clean.
- Python step definitions: features/steps/sort_steps.py — handles empty-string edge cases with explicit steps since behave regex can't match empty quoted values
- Rust: sort.rs implemented with SortCondition enum + OrderedValue newtype (implements Ord for serde_json::Value). 100% coverage via unit tests.
- Rust cucumber steps added to tests/cucumber.rs using regex= attribute to handle parametric operators
- parity checker failing: regex= steps in Rust not matched by parity checker pattern

Next: fix parity checker to extract regex= raw-string patterns from Rust steps, then run make check.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Phase 1 complete and landed. make check fully green: Python 100%, Rust 100%, parity 13/13 steps. Parity checker updated to handle Rust regex= raw string attributes and Python u'...' prefixes. Committed and pushed.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Phase 1 complete and landed. make check fully green: Python 100%, Rust 100%, parity 13/13 steps. Parity checker updated to handle Rust regex= raw string attributes and Python u'...' prefixes. Committed and pushed.

issues.description: Given a sort condition with any DynamoDB-style operator (eq, ne, lt, lte, gt, gte, between, begins_with, contains), when evaluated against a value, it should return the correct boolean result. Handles edge cases: null values, numeric vs string comparison, empty strings. Spec: features/sort/sort_conditions.feature
issues.title: Sort condition operators

issues.description: Given concurrent deletes and association resolution, the system should return either a valid result or clean null/empty, never a dangling reference or crash. Spec: features/concurrency/referential_integrity_under_load.feature
issues.title: Referential integrity under load

issues.description: Implement standalone predicate evaluators matching DynamoDB KeyConditionExpression operators: eq, ne, lt, lte, gt, gte, between, begins_with, contains. No dependencies on other modules. See Vision.md for full details.
issues.title: Phase 1: Sort Conditions

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text:
Phase 0 scaffold complete. All deliverables implemented:
- VERSION file (0.1.0)
- Python package: pyproject.toml, behave.ini, .coveragerc, src/virtuus/__init__.py, src/virtuus/_python/__init__.py
- Shared BDD: features/environment.py, features/steps/smoke_steps.py (7 steps covering all 3 smoke scenarios)
- Rust crate: Cargo.toml, src/lib.rs (version() + #[cfg(test)] unit tests), src/bin/virtuus.rs, tests/cucumber.rs
- tools/check_spec_parity.py (checks asymmetry, not completeness)
- Makefile with check, coverage-python, coverage-rust, check-parity, build targets
- .github/workflows/ci.yml
- AGENTS.md updated with code quality standards (Black+Ruff+D rules, Sphinx docstrings, 100% coverage)
- Ruff D rules enabled; module docstring added to virtuus/__init__.py

make check result: Python 100% coverage, Rust 100% coverage, parity 7/7 steps, all lint clean.

issues.description: Implement the Phase 0 project skeleton: VERSION file, Python package (pyproject.toml, behave, coverage), Rust crate (Cargo.toml, lib.rs, CLI bin, cucumber integration tests), shared step definitions in features/steps/, tools/check_spec_parity.py, Makefile, and CI workflow. All make coverage-python, make coverage-rust, and make check-parity must pass at 100%.
issues.title: Phase 0 scaffolding implementation

issues.description: Given a table loaded from a directory, when files on disk change (modified, added, deleted), the cache should detect staleness via two-tier mtime checking. JIT refresh happens automatically before query results are returned. Configurable: check_interval prevents re-checking within the interval; auto_refresh=False disables JIT refresh, requiring explicit warm() calls. Spec: features/cache/cache_invalidation.feature
issues.title: Cache invalidation

issues.description: Given readers and writers operating simultaneously, readers should never see partially-written records and writers should never corrupt the index for concurrent readers. Spec: features/concurrency/concurrent_read_write.feature
issues.title: Concurrent read-write

issues.description: Given files changing on disk while queries are in flight, readers should get either the old state or the new state, never a mix. JIT refresh + concurrent reads must be safe. Spec: features/concurrency/concurrent_refresh.feature
issues.title: Concurrent refresh

issues.description: Given a table, when I put/get/delete/upsert records, the table should maintain correct state. Supports scan, all, and bulk load operations. Also supports composite primary keys (partition + sort key): put/get/delete with composite PK, and two records with same partition but different sort keys coexist. Spec: features/core/table_crud.feature

issues.description: Implement Database container with nested dict query interface (execute), cursor-based pagination, field projection, database-level describe/validate diagnostics, and declarative YAML schema loading. See Vision.md for full details.
issues.title: Phase 6: Database + execute() + Schema-from-YAML

issues.description: Given a database under concurrent write load, when N threads call put() simultaneously, every record should be persisted with no lost writes, corrupted files, or duplicate PKs. Concurrent puts to the same PK use last-write-wins: no error, final state is one of the written values. Spec: features/concurrency/concurrent_writes.feature
issues.title: Concurrent writes

issues.description: Given a table backed by a directory, when I put a record it should be written to a JSON file on disk. Filename convention: {pk}.json for simple PKs, {partition}__{sort}.json for composite PKs. PKs containing path separators (/ or \) are rejected. When I delete, the file should be removed. Load reads all JSON files from the directory. All writes use atomic temp+rename. Non-JSON files are ignored. Spec: features/core/table_file_persistence.feature
issues.title: Table file persistence

issues.description: Given a large result set, when I execute() with limit and next_token directives, it should return paginated results with cursor-based pagination matching the DynamoDB pattern. Supports descending sort direction: pagination with sort_direction desc traverses in correct reverse order. Full traversal yields all records with no duplicates. Spec: features/query/execute_pagination.feature
issues.title: Execute paginated queries
